key_insight: Bayesian probability unifies forward and inverse uncertainty quantification in mechanics, enabling both parameter inference and surrogate construction under one framework.
engineering_relevance: For solid mechanics and biomechanics, this provides a principled way to manage experimental variability, calibrate models, and make predictions with quantified uncertainty.
method: Treat forward UQ as uncertainty propagation from inputs to outputs.
Treat inverse UQ as Bayesian parameter identification/calibration from measurements.
Use surrogate models, model selection, and experimental design to reduce the cost of inference and prediction.
evidence_signal: The abstract is a conceptual/theoretical synthesis rather than a new benchmark study, and it argues that surrogates are needed because direct uncertainty propagation can require too many model evaluations.
watch_item: Bayesian workflows can be computationally demanding and their results depend on prior choices, surrogate fidelity, and data quality.
paper_title: Uncertainty quantification in mechanics: A unified Bayesian perspective
